mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL levels signal accelerating inflation expectations, prompting equity investors to demand higher risk premiums and compressing forward discounted cash flows, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of high-inflation observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in UNRATE exceeding 0.5 percentage points indicates sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in RSAFS and HOUST signals robust consumer spending and housing market strength, which sustains corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining INDPRO levels during a monetary tightening cycle reflect constrained industrial output facing higher financing costs, which suppresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to positive during tightening periods, or 20-day forward returns show no negative bias when INDPRO declines alongside rising rates.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding PAYEMS levels reflect strengthening employment conditions that boost household income and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows, or 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of employment expansion months.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained FEDFUNDS level below 0.25% indicates extreme monetary accommodation that lowers discount rates and fuels equity risk appetite, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of ultra-low rate periods, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising GDPC1 and falling RSAFS indicates supply chain bottlenecks constraining consumer spending despite nominal growth, which triggers profit margin compression and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern yields positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid INDPRO expansion during a late-cycle expansion phase signals overheating industrial activity that precedes central bank intervention, causing immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of late-cycle industrial surges, or the signal fails to predict returns when controlling for concurrent monetary policy shifts.
